meta.title: TechPulse
meta.tagline: Tech & AI news, hot takes, and who’s saying what — from the builders you follow
meta.note: Built from your X Following timeline, filtered to tech/AI builders by profile. Click any story to see every take and what different people are saying.

stories.title: GLM 5.2 is the open model everyone is testing
stories.summary: A wave of builders are putting the new open-weight GLM 5.2 through its paces, comparing it to Opus and shipping it into tools. Common thread: open models are catching up fast and are way cheaper.

takes.name: Lee Robinson
takes.text:
You can now try GLM 5.2 in Cursor!

Excited to see more useful open models, thank you to Fireworks for partnering here. Results from our evals ↓ https://t.co/aUxnRtaRks

takes.name: François Chollet
takes.text: This is the strongest ARC-AGI-2 performance to date by an open-source model.

takes.name: Hassan
takes.text:
Announcing GLM Arena!

A series of tests (infographics, svgs, sites, ect..) ran on GLM 5.2 and Opus 4.8, with prompts included.

On average, GLM 5.2 produced 2x the tokens but was still faster + 3x cheaper with similar quality. https://t.co/Ifmz7tI3KN

takes.name: Hassan
takes.text:
Ran 10 more tests comparing GLM 5.2 & Opus.

On average, GLM 5.2 produced 2x the tokens but was still faster + 3x cheaper with similar quality!

I'm open sourcing all these tests tomorrow, including the code, my prompts, and the token/cost stats. https://t.co/RURYboxENK

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: Really fast GLM now live

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
GLM 5.2 Fast via Wafer is now available exclusively on Vercel AI Gateway.

2x faster token throughput vs other providers in internal benchmarks.

𝚖𝚘𝚍𝚎𝚕: '𝚣𝚊𝚒/𝚐𝚕𝚖-𝟻.𝟸-𝚏𝚊𝚜𝚝'

https://t.co/Ncp7vOpccC

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: open models are the future

takes.name: Mirko Monti
takes.text:
Chinese models are good on most benchmarks but... in real life are always with a 6 month delay.

that's cool. having a Fable models from china in 6 month would be crazy. But they are still behind by a lot.

stories.title: "Skill Hell": the AI skills & prompting debate
stories.summary: Matt Pocock kicked off a viral debate about bloated "skill" files full of vague instructions like "be thorough". Kent C. Dodds and others piled in on whether prompt phrasing actually changes model behavior.

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
Take a look at your favourite skill.

Go on, take a look.

Check for lines like:

- "Make the commit message very detailed"
- "Be thorough"
- "Make the implementation easy to read"

What do these lines have in common?

They're no-ops. They do nothing to change the agent's

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
2023: Tutorial Hell
2026: Skill Hell

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text:
Hello, developers, look at your skills, now back to mine, now back at your skills, now back to mine.
Sadly, they aren't mine, but if you stopped listening to hype-driven thought leaders and switched to durable skills, they could be like mine.
Look down, back up, where are you? https://t.co/YQpYQxbyCe

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text:
@threepointone @mattpocockuk Agreed. I could easily demonstrate each of these phrases actually changing the model's behavior.

Maybe what Matt is saying is that these specific examples don't change it meaningfully in most contexts?

takes.name: trash
takes.text: i feel this in my soul

takes.name: dex
takes.text: @mattpocockuk @kentcdodds @threepointone wait til my dude learns about “make no mistakes ultrathink”

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: prompting is the new coding

stories.title: Anthropic's "Fable" watch & the GPT-5.6 countdown
stories.summary: The community is impatiently tracking when Anthropic's Fable model returns, tied to speculation about GPT-5.6 dropping and complaints that current Anthropic models feel slow.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: Okay but seriously, where the hell is Fable? https://t.co/qwgwO0UvnX

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text:
Only one more day till GPT 5.6?! 

It’s about time. 

It’d be smart of Anthropic to wait till after 5.6 is released to give access back to Fable 5 

(saying they have figured government stuff out and now they’re waiting to get the news coverage AGAIN)

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: anthropic models are smart but feel slower than they should be

takes.name: spidey
takes.text: imagine Fable drops and your company already has Runlayer wired up, it’s genuinely over for all the manual work https://t.co/mbrAzP2KQE

stories.title: JS tooling ships: Bun 1.4, Astro Sätteri, Turborepo 2.10
stories.summary: A busy week for JavaScript infrastructure releases across runtimes, build systems, and rendering engines.

takes.name: Jarred Sumner
takes.text: Bun v1.4 ships July 7th https://t.co/OKLI48i5lJ

takes.name: Astro
takes.text:
Introducing Sätteri —  High-performance Markdown & MDX processing for the JavaScript ecosystem.

https://t.co/nLkscI6l6H https://t.co/urZKMpCQHe

takes.name: Astro
takes.text: Sätteri is built in Rust and maintained separately by one of our core contributors. It lives outside of the Astro org, but is now a core dependency of the project and contributes up to 60% faster builds in Astro 7.

takes.name: Turborepo
takes.text:
Turborepo 2.10

• Graceful shutdown
• Deferred task hashing
• Combine --𝚊𝚏𝚏𝚎𝚌𝚝𝚎𝚍 and --𝚏𝚒𝚕𝚝𝚎𝚛
• Local cache eviction settings

https://t.co/iNgIwCcBOj

stories.title: What's the real moat in the AI era?
stories.summary: With AI as table stakes, builders debate what actually creates defensibility now: trust, taste, and knowing what NOT to build.

takes.name: Thomas Trimoreau
takes.text:
Hot take:

The biggest moat today

isn’t AI.

It’s trust.

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: knowing what not to build is the moat

takes.name: Thomas Trimoreau
takes.text:
Everyone has AI now.

 That edge expired fast 

 What’s the new edge?

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text:
AI will bring forth an unprecedented surge in entrepreneurship.

From 'solopreneurs,' to the revitalization of the small & medium business segment, to the emergence of the largest companies of our times… providing the foundation of it all.

stories.title: Are AI agents actually replacing jobs?
stories.summary: Skeptical takes pushing back on hype: name one job actually replaced, and a warning that token-usage flexing hides real dependency on subscriptions.

takes.name: Thomas Trimoreau
takes.text:
They say AI will replace every job.

Name one already replaced.

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
there's something weird about everyone saying how productive their token usage is

but being entirely dependent on subscriptions and completely incapable of paying per token

is it providing returns or not?

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: if someone is talking about token efficiency in every sentence, they’re basically broke

stories.title: Figma's Config launches (Motion + more)
stories.summary: Figma shipped a batch of launches at Config including Figma Motion. Designers debate whether it just replaced video editors and how Figma keeps nailing the "fast follower" playbook.

takes.name: Ridd 🤿
takes.text:
what. a. Config. 😮

I interviewed the new Chief Design Officer @loredanacrisan to go behind the scenes of today's @figma launches

I think you'll really enjoy this one 👇 https://t.co/bJts22eLhI

takes.name: Ridd 🤿
takes.text:
kinda feels like @figma is on an Apple-esque timeline

They weren't the first to do any of this

But damn did they do it well 👏

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text:
This just replaced web designers and video editors for the 379th time supposedly lol

Jokes aside, this is geninuely insane as right now AI struggles tremendously with UI design let alone being able to make your own custom motion graphics

I will also 100% be adding this to my

stories.title: AI video generation goes full 4K
stories.summary: Higgsfield showed off Seedance 2.0 in 4K and a hybrid-produced AI animated film, while new video-generation APIs aimed at agents appeared.

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
We just made Higgsfield's first AI animated film, Kök Börü.

The first film made through hybrid production, where Seedance 2.0 in 4K and traditional animation work as one.

We are open-sourcing it all, every keyframe and prompt. Trailer below. https://t.co/7jQkv3FoWQ

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Seedance 2.0 in 4K brings back that launch-day excitement.

A full AI feature film is coming in 4K soon. https://t.co/SL6Q7LUNOV

takes.name: trash
takes.text: thought this was @im_roy_lee

stories.title: Why no framework ever dethroned React
stories.summary: Dax (thdxr) argued it is worth studying why React was never replaced, calling it deeply misunderstood. Kent C. Dodds felt vindicated.

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
it's worth deeply studying why no framework dethroned react

it's completely misunderstood and it's why every prediction you see by programmers tends to be wrong

and once you get it, you can apply this understanding to nearly everything you do

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text: @thdxr I just feel vindicated

stories.title: MCP's architecture growing pains
stories.summary: Dax flagged that MCP is too tied to "1 process = 1 session", sparking talk of shared gateways for multi-agent setups.

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
mcp is so tied to the idea of 1 process = 1 session

there's some things in the spec that force you to spawn a new MCP server for every active session you have

ridiculous overhead - this is why i kept saying it's too early to standardize

takes.name: Daniel Smith | Building ClawQL Agents

stories.title: Epic's app-store war: Google & Apple fees fall
stories.summary: Tim Sweeney is tracking major shifts: Google Play cutting fees to 10-15%, Android opening to competing stores, and a pending Supreme Court decision on Apple.

takes.name: Tim Sweeney
takes.text:
Google just launched a new structure for Google Play service fees, now as low as 10% for small businesses, and 15% for developers in their Level Up program for optimized Android games and apps!

https://t.co/a727hjoiLt

takes.name: Tim Sweeney
takes.text: Now Android is the most open a mobile platform has been in the era of ubiquitous smartphones, fully supporting competing stores with no junk fees, no scare screens for registered stores, and competing payments in-app and out-of-app.

takes.name: Tim Sweeney
takes.text:
Soon, the Supreme Court will rule on Apple's appeal, and Apple's will outline their proposed compliance proposal for the injunction the District Court found they violated.

As UE6 and a new era of gaming approach, we're in the final battle for open competition and open markets!

stories.title: Lee Robinson leaves Vercel for Cursor to train models
stories.summary: One of the biggest individual moves: leerob announced he is joining Cursor to move into ML and research model behavior (training Composer).

takes.name: Lee Robinson
takes.text:
Personal update, I'm starting a new role at Cursor!

I'm moving into ML, working on training Composer. I'll be researching how to improve model behavior and personality.

hotTakes.name: dax
hotTakes.text:
i found a really good way to measure how much a codebase is suffering from vibe coding

rg -o 'isRecord' . | wc -l

hotTakes.name: Can Vardar
hotTakes.text: being famous on tech twitter is like buying nvidia stock in 2015

hotTakes.name: matt palmer
hotTakes.text: coworking is the adult equivalent of "studying" with your friends at the library

hotTakes.name: Steve Ruiz
hotTakes.text: it's too hot out to build the app

hotTakes.name: dex
hotTakes.text: name a more user hostile decision than publishing a cli in 2026 that hardcodes an output color to ANSII black instead of just the themes FG color

hotTakes.name: Can Vardar
hotTakes.text: knowing what not to build is the moat
